Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 089. The Canal Inspector in Assur (ABL 0562)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL] EN-[ia]

(1) [To the king, my] lord: Your servant [Ṭab-ṣill-Ešarra]. Good health [to the king, my lord!] May Aššur and Mullissu bless the [king], my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka m[DÙG.GA—ṣil—É.ŠÁR.RA]

o 33

lu DI-[mu a-na LUGAL EN-ia]

o 44

aš-šur d⸢NIN.LÍL⸣ a-na [LUGAL]

o 55

EN-ia lik-ru-[bu]

o 66

ina UGU mEN—ḫa-[x x]

(6) As to Bel-ha[...], the canal inspector of whom the king, my lord, wr[ote to me],

o 77

LÚv.GÚ.⸢GAL⸣

o 88

ša LUGAL EN iš-[pur-an-ni]

o 99

UD 04-KÁM ina [x x]

(9) he arrived in the Inner City on the [eve] of the 4th, brought out his ... and left for the [king, my lord].

o 1010

ina URU.ŠÀ—URU

o 1111

iq-ṭar-⸢ba⸣

o 1212

qar-ba-te-⸢šu⸣

o 1313

ú-se-ṣi-a

r 1r 1

ina pa-an [LUGAL]

r 22

i-ta-[lak]

rest uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334384/.
